data.epi_content:
"What on earth are you doing right now?!"

Vivian instantly gathered her magical energy and began to chant a spell.

Though she didn't know why, looking at the two bound girls and Evan who seemed to radiate an incredibly evil aura, she felt she had to act.

No dialogue was necessary.

Black magicians must be burned to death.

Or they should be chopped into pieces so small they couldn't be recognized as human and fed to livestock as feed.

That's how they must be punished.

Or they should be obliterated on the spot with powerful magic that leaves no trace.

That was common sense for those living in this world.

But what if that black magician was someone you knew?

What if it was someone you were dating, someone you hadn't broken up with yet, someone who had taken your first kiss?

When told that a person who is clearly human—who felt the same joys, happiness, and sorrows, who awakened something akin to love—is not human, most people cannot readily accept it.

And Vivian belonged to that majority.

"Evan, you know this is madness. Do you really think this will bring dead Erica back to life?!"

At those words, Evan put the cigar that Erica had been smoking in her final moments between his lips and lit it.

Sitting on a round chair with his legs crossed, he looked at Vivian.

After taking a drag and savoring the aroma, he said mockingly:

"Listen, Vivian. Erica isn't dead."

"No, she is. She burned to death right before my eyes, the flames impossible to extinguish no matter how hard I tried."

"No, she's alive. From the day she slapped your cheek."

"What, are you trying to say that's when Erica died? Erica! I killed her! And you killed her! She's been dead for a long time!!"

"Maybe so. But I'm telling you she's alive."

"Get a grip, Evan.

We both saw it. Erica dying in flames that wouldn't extinguish no matter how much water or dirt we threw on them."

"Yes, we saw it, and I realized!

Every time Erica dies, she goes back to the past."

Hearing this, Vivian's eyes grew cold.

Until now, she had been trying to comfort someone walking down the wrong path after experiencing loss, trying to bring them back to normal. Now she was simply looking at a madman.

"Untie those children."

"Well, Vivian, if we burned Erica to death, these children are probably responsible for her tongue being cut into pieces before she died.

Vivian, you were the one who took Erica to class and brought her back.

Even so, you didn't know she was being bullied?"

Surprisingly, Vivian didn't know.

And Evan knew that she didn't know.

In her mind, Erica had always looked like a sick chicken, and whether the chicken looked more lively one day or sicker another, a sick chicken was a sick chicken.

Even though she kept her in a cage, close enough to feel her breath, the stupid owner had no way of knowing since the caged chicken never said she was in pain.

She was just too optimistic and positive about the world.

But he shouldn't have denied it.

Denying something you failed to notice was a kind of sin to Evan, who studied and understood people.

"That can't be true, you're obviously making this up..."
data.epi_content2:
"Some days she'd come with the hem of her uniform slightly wet, other days she'd be fine in the morning but by lunchtime her eyes would be dull and lifeless, and sometimes the inside of her mouth would be completely raw.

I thought maybe she was stressed from being around you, maybe that's why she had mouth sores, but no.

These friends were just bullying her diligently. Just careful enough not to let me catch them."

When had it happened?

The bound children, forgetting the cloth in their mouths, tried to scream for help—mmph, mmph—though with their mouths covered, the sounds merely slipped sideways.

"But Vivian, why didn't you know?"

Vivian could only close her mouth, trembling and crying.

Thinking about it now, it seemed Erica had indeed been bullied.

But she had been blaming herself, thinking only she and Evan were the ones tormenting her. The self-loathing of not noticing other people bullying Erica and being unable to defend herself when confronted with this fact left her only able to tremble and cry.

"Well, I'm not going to yell at you or anything.

We're not teacher and student or blood relatives. We're lovers, even if only in name."

"MMMMPH!!"

Evan removed the cigar from his mouth as if discarding its taste, and extinguished it by burning the face of the student sitting in front of him.

The smell of cooking flesh began to spread faintly through the room.

Evan smiled, and Vivian frowned, glaring at him.

"This is about saving Erica, so it's about saving a person.

Hey, that's a good thing. Listen, Vivian. I called you because I could use your help.

I may not know the theory, but the spells..."

"That's enough nonsense."

Seeing this, Vivian's expression became solemn, as if she had made up her mind. She created a sharp icicle and shot it toward Evan's hand.

The sharp icicle seemed about to pierce through Evan's hand but stopped, embedded in it.

Though he should have screamed, he acted as if nothing had happened, pulling out the icicle and stabbing it into the head of the student whose face he had burned.

Watching this, Vivian could only cry.

If she had just aimed the sharp ice at Evan's forehead, she could have killed him.

But she couldn't bring herself to do it.

She was indecisive.

Unable to make choices no matter what happened.

That's why she killed Erica.

But what had happened to Evan to change him so much?

The dead are dead.

Though she couldn't spend her life blaming herself for her sin, committing another sin and defiling the already dead seemed unspeakably horrible.

"Oh dear, Vivian. You've killed another person."

Then, as if mad with fear, he grabbed the hair of the student who was frantically blinking her right eye after having her left eye gouged out, lifting her up like a hostage.

It was effective.

It made Vivian scatter the spell she was about to cast into the air.

"Why not? If we've already killed two people, what's one more?"

"Erica is already dead.

Do you really think doing this will bring her back to life?

She was in so much pain that she burned without even screaming, just smiling as she went up in flames.

Every time I try to sleep, every time I close my eyes, every time I get tired and my vision blurs slightly, I see her.

Each time, I curl up and just repeat, I'm sorry, I'm sorry.
data.epi_content3:
But I never thought about trying to resurrect Erica."

"You talk too much."

"I'm not a madman like you, and I prefer not to insult the dead by trying to resurrect them."

"Listen, Vivian. We didn't kill Erica just once.

It might sound crazy, but Erica is living in the past again right now.

I don't know exactly how it happened, but I guess Duke Mecklenburg sacrificed tens of thousands of slaves from his farm or something."

"So you have no intention of stopping this?"

"None. In fact, it would be better if you helped me."

"Stop this nonsensical garbage."

"So that's why."

Evan muttered something to himself, then melted the woman he was holding in his hand.

Vivian watched this horrific scene calmly, as if she had known this would happen.

At the same time, she drew magic power into her hands and rushed at Evan.

Combat between magicians is extremely simple.

Only in war do they recite grand magical incantations before dropping them on enemy camps. In small spaces like this, it all came down to close combat.

And Vivian, having a natural talent for magic, could cast simple spells in less than a second.

Vivian threw a fireball, and Evan blocked the flames by lifting the hair of the melting flesh.

"Oh dear, this was alive too.

Vivian, you've killed another person."

"Shut up! You crazy...!!"

Evan inserted a bead that rose from the flesh into his hand where the icicle had been embedded.

Simultaneously, the flesh became mushy and the wound disappeared without a trace.

Vivian tore off a piece of the wall, hardened it with a spell, and swung it like a sword at Evan's head.

She channeled wind into her fist and punched his jaw, and seeing him lose balance, she leaped into the air and kicked his face.

The bookish scholar who only knew how to read and research was incredibly weak.

Even if he cast curses, it only made her already unpleasant mood slightly worse, and if he tried to melt her body, she could simply dodge.

Moreover, Evan didn't want to kill Vivian.

Despite his resentment, the memories of happily spending time with Erica and Vivian were his happiest memories.

Probably a time that would never return.

Vivian literally beat Evan's face to a pulp with the club she had made from the wall.

As blood spurted, teeth shattered, and his nose broke repeatedly, when he was barely breathing, Evan whispered:

"Kill me, like Erica. Preferably by burning me."

Though his face was caved in, his voice flowed perfectly from his mouth.

Looking at this sight, Vivian couldn't kill him and only dropped tears.

Cursing her cursed life.

She ran away again.

As always.
